code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a written work into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Clean object values (remove surrounding quotes if present)
    def clean_object(obj: str) -> str:
        if obj.startswith('"') and obj.endswith('"'):
            return obj[1:-1]
        return obj

    # Simple templates for common predicates
    templates = {
        "author": "written by {object}",
        "isbnNumber": "with ISBN number {object}",
        "followedBy": "followed by {object}",
        "precededBy": "preceded by {object}",
        "language": "written in {object}",
        "mediaType": "in {object} format",
        "numberOfPages": "with {object} pages",
        "country": "published in {object}",
        "publisher": "published by {object}",
        "genre": "a {object} work",
        "literaryGenre": "in the {object} genre",
        "releaseDate": "released on {object}",
        "birthDate": "born on {object}",
        "deathDate": "died on {object}",
        "birthPlace": "born in {object}",
        "deathPlace": "died in {object}",
        "almaMater": "educated at {object}",
        "doctoralAdvisor": "advised by {object}",
        "nationality": "of {object} nationality",
        "residence": "residing in {object}",
        "codenCode": "with CODEN code {object}",
        "issnNumber": "ISSN {object}",
        "eissnNumber": "e-ISSN {object}",
        "LCCN_number": "LCCN {object}",
        "abbreviation": "abbreviated as {object}",
        "academicDiscipline": "focusing on {object}",
        "firstPublicationYear": "first published in {object}",
        "editor": "edited by {object}",
        "impactFactor": "with an impact factor of {object}",
        "oclcNumber": "OCLC number {object}",
        "libraryofCongressClassification": "classified as {object}",
        "frequency": "published {object}",
        "headquarter": "headquartered in {object}",
        "leader": "led by {object}",
        "city": "located in {object}",
        "state": "in {object} state",
        "spokenIn": "spoken in {object}",
        "influencedBy": "influenced by {object}",
        "notableWork": "known for {object}",
        "regionServed": "serving {object}",
        "founder": "founded by {object}",
        "capital": "capital is {object}",
        "demonym": "known as {object}",
        "leaderTitle": "leader title is {object}",
        "nickname": "nicknamed {object}",
        "affiliation": "affiliated with {object}",
        "parentCompany": "owned by {object}",
        "location": "located in {object}",
        "ethnicGroup": "ethnic group {object}",
        "largestCity": "largest city is {object}",
        "family": "family {object}",
        "genus": "genus {object}",
    }

    # Group predicates by subject for better sentence structure
    subject_data = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_data:
            subject_data[t.subject] = []
        subject_data[t.subject].append(t)

    # Build clauses for each subject
    all_clauses = []
    for subject, subject_triples in subject_data.items():
        subject_clauses = []
        for t in subject_triples:
            tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
            obj = clean_object(t.object)
            if tmpl:
                clause = tmpl.format(object=obj)
                subject_clauses.append(clause)
            else:
                subject_clauses.append(f"{t.predicate} {obj}")

        # Combine subject clauses with the subject name
        if subject_clauses:
            if len(subject_clauses) == 1:
                clause_text = f"{subject} {subject_clauses[0]}"
            else:
                clause_text = f"{subject} {'; '.join(subject_clauses[:-1])}, and {subject_clauses[-1]}"
            all_clauses.append(clause_text)

    # Combine all subject clauses
    if len(all_clauses) == 1:
        sentence = all_clauses[0]
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(all_clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + all_clauses[-1]

    # Ensure proper capitalization and punctuation
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:] + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
